title: feat: LLMSettings env parsing — provider/model/api_key/api_base
description:
Implement LLMSettings in src/pypsa_app/llm/settings.py with the four core provider fields. Per backend-spec.md §2.1.

Fields (all read from environment, NO prefix, exact aliases shown):
  llm_provider: str       alias=LLM_PROVIDER       default="openai"
  llm_model:    str       alias=LLM_MODEL          default="qwen3.5:9b"
  llm_api_key:  str       alias=LLM_API_KEY        default=""
  llm_api_base: str|None  alias=LLM_API_BASE       default=None

Use pydantic_settings.BaseSettings with `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="", extra="ignore")`.

RED: tests/llm/test_settings.py with cases that monkeypatch env vars (LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic, LLM_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6, LLM_API_KEY=sk-ant-..., LLM_API_BASE=https://...) and assert each field reads correctly. Add a test asserting defaults when env unset.
GREEN: minimal pydantic-settings model with just these four fields.

title: feat: LLMSettings — generation params (max_tokens, temperature, timeout)
description:
Add generation parameters to LLMSettings. Per backend-spec.md §2.1.

Fields:

  llm_temperature:              float alias=LLM_TEMPERATURE              default=0.2
  llm_request_timeout_seconds:  float alias=LLM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS  default=120.0

RED: tests covering env-var override and defaults; assert types (int / float).
GREEN: extend the LLMSettings model with the three Field(...) declarations.

title: feat: LLMSettings — chat_enabled + max_tool_iterations
description:
Add the chat feature flag and tool-iteration cap. Per backend-spec.md §2.1 + Q9.

Fields:
  chat_enabled:             bool alias=CHAT_ENABLED             default=False
  llm_max_tool_iterations:  int  alias=LLM_MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS  default=8

RED: tests/llm/test_settings.py asserting (a) default chat_enabled is False, (b) CHAT_ENABLED=true / 1 / yes all parse as truthy, (c) max_tool_iterations defaults to 8 and accepts override.
GREEN: two more Field(...) declarations on LLMSettings.

title: feat: LLMSettings.model_string property
description:
Implement LLMSettings.model_string property returning `{provider}/{model}`. Per backend-spec.md §2.1 / D2.

RED: tests covering openai/qwen3.5:9b, anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 (note: nested slash is fine — LiteLLM treats everything after the first slash as model id).
GREEN: `@property def model_string(self) -> str: return f"{self.llm_provider}/{self.llm_model}"`.

Critical reminder for the implementer (not a unit test, but document it in the property docstring): the `openai/` prefix MUST be used for Ollama models (NOT `ollama/`) — the latter has a LiteLLM bug that drops tool_calls for reasoning models. This was validated 2026-05-04 against qwen3.5:9b.

title: feat: LLMSettings.llm_internal_api_base default-from-port
description:
Implement the `llm_internal_api_base` field with a default-from-port behavior. Per backend-spec.md §2.1 + §7.3.

Field:
  llm_internal_api_base: str|None  alias=LLM_INTERNAL_API_BASE  default=None

When unset, callers must derive `http://127.0.0.1:{settings.port}` at runtime — settings.port comes from the top-level Settings (BACK-A-007 composes them).

RED: test that LLM_INTERNAL_API_BASE=http://foo:1234 is honored; test that None default is preserved (the default-from-port logic lives in build_internal_http_client, BACK-A-025, not here).
GREEN: one Field declaration.

title: feat: compose LLMSettings into top-level Settings
description:
Compose LLMSettings into the top-level Settings class. Per backend-spec.md §2.2.

In src/pypsa_app/backend/settings.py add:
  from pypsa_app.llm.settings import LLMSettings
  class Settings(BaseSettings):
      ...existing fields...
      llm: LLMSettings = LLMSettings()

The LLMSettings instance reads its OWN env vars (not nested) because aliases are explicit and have no prefix.

RED: tests/backend/test_settings.py asserting (a) `Settings().llm.chat_enabled is False` by default, (b) when CHAT_ENABLED=true env is set, `Settings().llm.chat_enabled is True`, (c) `Settings().llm.model_string == "openai/qwen3.5:9b"` by default.
GREEN: one import + one field on Settings.

Gotcha: do NOT put nested env_prefix on LLMSettings — it would change the alias semantics. Aliases stay flat at the env layer.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/events.py:

  @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)

These are PURE data — no SSE serialization yet (that's BACK-A-013).

  @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)

GREEN: two dataclasses.

title: feat: AGUIEvent dataclasses for ToolCallStart/Args/End/Result
feat: SSE serializer — event:/data: framing
description:
Implement the SSE serializer to_sse(event) -> bytes. Per backend-spec.md §3.2.

Signature: `def to_sse(event: object) -> bytes` where `event` is one of the dataclasses from 010/011/012.

Output format (no trailing whitespace before \n; blank line terminator is two \n):
  event: TextMessageContent\ndata: {"message_id": "msg_01", "delta": "Hello"}\n\n

Implementation:
  def to_sse(event) -> bytes:
      name = type(event).__name__
      payload = json.dumps(dataclasses.asdict(event), separators=(',',':'))
      return f"event: {name}\ndata: {payload}\n\n".encode()

RED: golden-bytes tests for each event type. Assert exact byte sequence including the double newline. Assert that JSON has no spaces (compact), so heartbeats parse predictably downstream.
GREEN: ~5-line function.

title: feat: SSE heartbeat helper (:keepalive\\n\\n every 15 s while idle)
description:
Implement the SSE heartbeat helper. Per backend-spec.md §3.2.

  def heartbeat() -> bytes:
      return b":keepalive\n\n"

The colon prefix marks it as an SSE comment — clients ignore it but it keeps the connection alive through proxies. The caller (ChatService) emits one every 15 s while idle.

RED: test asserting return value is exactly `b":keepalive\n\n"` and that it does not contain `event:` or `data:` prefixes.
GREEN: one-line function.

title: feat: SSE bytes round-trip for all 9 event types
description:
Round-trip integration test: every event type serializes via to_sse() and parses back via a tiny SSE block parser into a dict whose shape matches dataclasses.asdict(original_event). Per backend-spec.md §3.2 / §9.1.

Write the parser inline in the test (do not put it in production):
  def parse_block(block: bytes) -> tuple[str, dict]:
      lines = block.decode().split("\n")
      ev = next(l[6:].strip() for l in lines if l.startswith("event:"))
      data = next(l[5:].strip() for l in lines if l.startswith("data:"))
      return ev, json.loads(data)

RED: parametrize over all nine event types from §3.1. For each, build an instance, serialize, parse, assert (event_name, parsed_dict) matches.
GREEN: rely on existing to_sse — no production change needed if the test passes.

title: feat: Tool ABC + ToolContext + ToolResult dataclasses
description:
Define Tool ABC, ToolContext, ToolResult dataclasses. Per backend-spec.md §7.1.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/tools/base.py:
  @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
  class ToolContext:    user_id: str; auth_cookie: str|None
  @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
  class ToolResult:     payload: dict[str,Any]|None; is_error: bool=False; error: str|None=None
  class Tool(ABC):
      name: str
      description: str
      parameters_schema: dict[str, Any]
      def __init__(self, http: httpx.AsyncClient) -> None: self._http = http
      @abstractmethod
      async def invoke(self, args: dict[str,Any], ctx: ToolContext) -> ToolResult: ...

RED: test that creating a subclass without `name`/`description`/`parameters_schema`/`invoke` raises (or at least leaves the abstract method unimplemented). Test ToolResult default flags (is_error=False, error=None).
GREEN: the three definitions above.

Implement build_internal_http_client(settings) factory. Per backend-spec.md §7.3.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/tools/http_client.py:

      base = settings.llm.llm_internal_api_base or f"http://127.0.0.1:{settings.port}"
      return httpx.AsyncClient(base_url=base, timeout=30.0)

title: feat: tool auth-cookie forwarding helper
description:
Implement the auth-cookie forwarding helper for tools. Per backend-spec.md §7.3 + §4.2.

The chat route extracts the user's session cookie and stores it on ToolContext. Tools must forward it on outbound internal HTTP calls so the called endpoint sees the same authenticated user.

Helper:
  def cookies_for(ctx: ToolContext) -> dict[str, str] | None:
      return {"session": ctx.auth_cookie} if ctx.auth_cookie else None

RED: tests covering both branches (cookie present → dict; cookie None → None).
GREEN: one-line function.

Tools call self._http.get(..., cookies=cookies_for(ctx)) — they NEVER bypass this.

title: feat: list_networks tool — call /api/v1/networks
description:
Implement ListNetworksTool — calls GET /api/v1/networks. Per backend-spec.md §7.4.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/tools/list_networks.py:
  class ListNetworksTool(Tool):
      name = "list_networks"
      description = "List networks the user has access to. Returns name, id, owner, and last-modified for each."
      parameters_schema = {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {"limit": {"type":"integer","minimum":1,"maximum":100,"default":25}},
      }
      async def invoke(self, args, ctx):
          limit = args.get("limit", 25)
          r = await self._http.get("/api/v1/networks", params={"limit": limit}, cookies=cookies_for(ctx))
          r.raise_for_status()
          body = r.json()
          rows = [[n["id"], n["name"], n["owner"], n["modified"]] for n in body["items"]]
          return ToolResult(payload={
              "summary": f"Found {len(rows)} networks (limit {limit}).",
              "data": {"columns": ["id","name","owner","modified"], "rows": rows},
              "display_hint": "table",
          })

RED: tests using respx (or httpx mock transport) to fake /api/v1/networks responses. Assert payload shape includes summary/data/display_hint and rows reshape correctly. Assert HTTP error → raised (caught by registry — separate test).
GREEN: the class.

title: feat: get_network_detail tool — call /api/v1/networks/{id}
description:
Implement GetNetworkDetailTool — calls GET /api/v1/networks/{id}. Per backend-spec.md §7.4.

  class GetNetworkDetailTool(Tool):
      name = "get_network_detail"
      description = "Return the detail card for one network: id, name, owner, size, modified, optional metadata."
      parameters_schema = {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {"network_id": {"type":"string"}},
          "required": ["network_id"],
      }
      async def invoke(self, args, ctx):
          nid = args["network_id"]
          r = await self._http.get(f"/api/v1/networks/{nid}", cookies=cookies_for(ctx))
          r.raise_for_status()
          n = r.json()
          return ToolResult(payload={
              "summary": f"Network {n['name']} ({n['id']}) — {n.get('size_kb','?')} KB, modified {n['modified']}.",
              "data": n,
              "display_hint": "table",
          })

RED: respx-mocked /api/v1/networks/abc → asserts payload.summary references the name and id.
GREEN: the class.

title: feat: get_network_statistics tool — payload with chart_spec + display_hint

description:
Wire the three tools into a default ToolRegistry via build_default_registry(http). Per backend-spec.md §7.2.

  def build_default_registry(http: httpx.AsyncClient) -> ToolRegistry:
      return ToolRegistry([
          ListNetworksTool(http),
          GetNetworkDetailTool(http),
          GetNetworkStatisticsTool(http),
      ])

RED: test that the returned registry resolves all three names and schemas() returns three entries with the right `function.name` values.
GREEN: one function.

title: feat: LiteLLM client.stream() — openai/ prefix routing
description:
Implement LLMClient.stream() routing through LiteLLM with the openai/ prefix. Per backend-spec.md §5.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/client.py:
  litellm.drop_params = True

  class LLMClient:
      def __init__(self, settings: LLMSettings) -> None: self._s = settings
      async def stream(self, messages, tools=None):
          kwargs = {
              "model": self._s.model_string,
              "messages": messages,
              "max_tokens": self._s.llm_max_tokens,
              "temperature": self._s.llm_temperature,
              "stream": True,
              "timeout": self._s.llm_request_timeout_seconds,
          }
          if self._s.llm_api_key:  kwargs["api_key"]  = self._s.llm_api_key
          if self._s.llm_api_base: kwargs["api_base"] = self._s.llm_api_base
          if tools:                kwargs["tools"]    = tools
          async for chunk in await litellm.acompletion(**kwargs):
              yield chunk

RED: tests/llm/test_client.py monkeypatching litellm.acompletion to record kwargs; assert (a) model="openai/qwen3.5:9b" composed from settings, (b) api_base passed through, (c) max_tokens/temperature/timeout passed, (d) `tools` omitted when None.
GREEN: the class above.

Reminder docstring: NEVER use `ollama/` prefix — even for Ollama models, use `openai/` because litellm's ollama adapter drops tool_calls for reasoning models.

title: feat: LLMClient.stream() — pass tools when provided
description:
Pass the tools list through to litellm.acompletion when provided. Per backend-spec.md §5.

This is mostly already done in 040, but a dedicated test ensures the kwargs flow is correct end-to-end:

RED: monkeypatch litellm.acompletion → record kwargs. Call client.stream(messages, tools=[{...openai schema...}]); assert recorded kwargs["tools"] is the same list. Call client.stream(messages); assert "tools" not in kwargs.
GREEN: ensure 040's `if tools: kwargs["tools"] = tools` is right.

title: feat: LLMClient.health() ping completion
description:
Implement LLMClient.health() pinging the provider with a 1-token completion. Per backend-spec.md §5.

  async def health(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
      kwargs = {"model": self._s.model_string, "messages": [{"role":"user","content":"ping"}], "max_tokens": 1}
      if self._s.llm_api_key:  kwargs["api_key"]  = self._s.llm_api_key
      if self._s.llm_api_base: kwargs["api_base"] = self._s.llm_api_base
      await litellm.acompletion(**kwargs)
      return {"ok": True, "model": self._s.model_string}

RED: monkeypatch litellm.acompletion → returns success → health() returns {"ok": True, "model": "openai/qwen3.5:9b"}. Failure → exception bubbles.
GREEN: the method.

title: feat: reasoning-content normalization across providers
description:
Implement reasoning-content normalization across providers. Per backend-spec.md §5 / §6.4.

Provider-specific delta fields:
  Anthropic   → chunk.choices[0].delta.thinking
  Ollama qwen → chunk.choices[0].delta.reasoning_content
  OpenAI o1   → folded into content; not separately streamed

Helper in src/pypsa_app/llm/client.py (or a new module):
  def extract_reasoning(delta) -> str:
      return getattr(delta, "thinking", None) or getattr(delta, "reasoning_content", None) or ""

  def extract_text(delta) -> str:
      return getattr(delta, "content", None) or ""

RED: tests with mock delta objects exposing each variant — assert extract_reasoning picks the right field. Test where neither attribute exists → returns "".
GREEN: two small helpers. ChatService (BACK-A-062) consumes these.

title: feat: prompts.BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT + build_system_prompt empty context
description:
Define BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT and build_system_prompt() with empty context. Per backend-spec.md §6.3.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/prompts.py:
  BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = '''\
  You are a copilot embedded in the PyPSA App, a tool for visualising power-system
  networks. You help users understand and analyse their networks.

  You have tools to inspect the user's networks. Prefer calling tools over guessing.
  When a user asks a quantitative question, look up the data — never invent numbers.

  When you call a tool, summarise the result in plain language. The UI will render
  charts and tables for the user separately; your text should explain *what* the
  data means, not just restate it.
  '''

  def build_system_prompt(context: ChatContext) -> str:
      parts = [BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT]
      # active network and pinned ids appended in 051/052
      return "\n\n".join(parts)

(ChatContext is the schema from BACK-A-080. For now define it as a stub Pydantic model with `active_network_id`, `active_network_name`, `pinned_network_ids: list[str]` so tests can run.)

RED: assert build_system_prompt(ChatContext()) == BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (no extra parts).
GREEN: function above.

title: feat: build_system_prompt with active network
description:
Extend build_system_prompt with the active-network deictic-reference fragment. Per backend-spec.md §6.3 + Q8.

When context.active_network_id and context.active_network_name are both set, append:

  The user is currently viewing network "<name>" (id: <id>). When they say
  "this network", "here", or use other deictic references, assume they mean
  that network unless they say otherwise. The user may ask about other
  networks at any time — use list_networks to discover them.

RED: build_system_prompt(ChatContext(active_network_id="n1", active_network_name="Test Net")) contains both the id and name, AND mentions list_networks. ChatContext() (no active) does NOT contain "this network".
GREEN: append a parts entry conditional on both fields being non-empty.

title: feat: build_system_prompt with pinned networks
description:
Append pinned-network ids to the system prompt. Per backend-spec.md §6.3 + Q5 forward-compat.

When `context.pinned_network_ids` is non-empty, append:

  Additional networks pinned to this conversation: <id1>, <id2>, ...

RED: ChatContext(pinned_network_ids=["a","b"]) → output contains "Additional networks pinned to this conversation: a, b". Empty list → no such fragment.
GREEN: one more conditional parts entry.

title: feat: ChatService skeleton — emits RunStarted + RunFinished only
description:
ChatService skeleton — emits RunStarted then RunFinished only (no tool loop yet). Per backend-spec.md §6.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/service.py:
  class ChatService:
      def __init__(self, client: LLMClient, tools: ToolRegistry, settings: LLMSettings): ...
      async def run_chat(self, *, user, messages, context, client_disconnected) -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
          run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
          yield to_sse(RunStarted(run_id=run_id, model=self._settings.model_string))
          # later: stream completions, run tool loop
          yield to_sse(RunFinished(run_id=run_id, usage={"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0}, stop_reason="stop"))

For this issue, a stub LLMClient is fine — just skip the actual completion.

RED: test that calls run_chat with empty messages list and a fake LLMClient whose stream() yields nothing. Collect bytes; parse; assert sequence is exactly [RunStarted, RunFinished] with matching run_id.
GREEN: the skeleton above.

title: feat: ChatService streams TextMessageContent deltas
description:
Stream TextMessageContent deltas as the LLM produces content chunks. Per backend-spec.md §6.2 / §6.4.

Inside run_chat (between RunStarted and RunFinished), iterate over `self._client.stream(messages, tools=...)`. For each chunk, extract `delta.content` via extract_text() (BACK-A-043). If non-empty, yield `to_sse(TextMessageContent(message_id=mid, delta=text))` where `mid` is generated once per assistant message.

RED: fake LLMClient.stream() that yields three chunks with content "Hel", "lo", " world". Run run_chat; assert event sequence: RunStarted, TextMessageContent("Hel"), TextMessageContent("lo"), TextMessageContent(" world"), RunFinished. Assert all TextMessageContent share the same message_id.
GREEN: extend run_chat with the streaming loop.

title: feat: ChatService streams ReasoningMessageContent deltas
description:
Stream ReasoningMessageContent deltas before text in the same chunk. Per backend-spec.md §6.4.

Reasoning is emitted FIRST when both reasoning and text are present in one chunk (so the client UI shows thinking → answer in order).

For each chunk:
  reasoning = extract_reasoning(delta)
  text      = extract_text(delta)
  if reasoning: yield to_sse(ReasoningMessageContent(message_id=mid, delta=reasoning))
  if text:      yield to_sse(TextMessageContent(message_id=mid, delta=text))

RED: fake stream yielding chunks with delta.reasoning_content="thinking" then delta.content="answer"; assert ReasoningMessageContent comes before TextMessageContent.
GREEN: insert reasoning emission before text in the chunk loop.

title: feat: ChatService — ToolCallStart on first chunk of a tool call
feat: ChatService — ToolCallArgs streaming
description:
Stream ToolCallArgs deltas as arguments JSON arrives. Per backend-spec.md §6.5.

For each tc_delta where function.arguments is non-empty:
  tool_buffers[tcid]["args_str"] += tc_delta.function.arguments
  yield to_sse(ToolCallArgs(tool_call_id=tcid, delta=tc_delta.function.arguments))

RED: fake stream with chunk 1 starting the call, chunk 2 adding `{"limit":` , chunk 3 adding `25}`. Assert two ToolCallArgs events with deltas `{"limit":` and `25}`.
GREEN: the args accumulation.

title: feat: ChatService — ToolCallEnd on finish_reason=tool_calls
feat: ChatService — invoke tool via registry, emit ToolCallResult
description:
Invoke the tool via the registry and emit ToolCallResult. Per backend-spec.md §6.5 / §6.2.

After ToolCallEnd, call:
  result = await self._tools.invoke(buf["name"], args, ToolContext(user_id=user.id, auth_cookie=...))
  yield to_sse(ToolCallResult(
      tool_call_id=tcid,
      result=result.payload,
      is_error=result.is_error,
      error=result.error,
  ))

RED: fake LLM emits one tool call to "list_networks". Stub the registry so it returns ToolResult(payload={"summary":"ok","data":{"columns":[],"rows":[]},"display_hint":"table"}). Assert ToolCallResult emitted with is_error=False and the payload propagated.
GREEN: integrate registry call.

title: feat: ChatService — append tool message + continue loop
description:
Append the tool message and continue the loop for a second iteration. Per backend-spec.md §6.2 (steps c.iv).

After ToolCallResult, append two messages to the running messages list:
  messages.append({"role":"assistant","tool_calls":[{"id":tcid,"type":"function","function":{"name":buf["name"],"arguments":buf["args_str"]}}]})
  messages.append({"role":"tool","tool_call_id":tcid,"content":json.dumps(result.payload)})

Then continue the outer for-iter loop with self._client.stream(messages, tools=...) called again.

RED: fake LLMClient.stream that on first call yields a tool_calls chunk + finish_reason="tool_calls", on second call yields a content chunk + finish_reason="stop". Assert event sequence: RunStarted, ToolCallStart, ToolCallArgs, ToolCallEnd, ToolCallResult, TextMessageContent, RunFinished.
GREEN: wrap the chunk loop in `for iter in range(max_tool_iterations)` and append+continue on tool_calls.

title: feat: ChatService — max_tool_iterations cap → RunError
description:
Cap tool iterations and emit RunError when the cap is hit. Per backend-spec.md §6.6.

If the for-iter loop exhausts settings.llm_max_tool_iterations rounds without finish_reason="stop", emit:
  yield to_sse(RunError(run_id=run_id, code="tool_iteration_limit", message="too many tool calls"))
  return

RED: fake LLM that emits tool_calls infinitely. With max_tool_iterations=2, assert RunError emitted with code="tool_iteration_limit" after the second iteration.
GREEN: post-loop check that emits RunError if no RunFinished was emitted.

title: feat: ChatService — invalid tool args JSON → ToolCallResult(is_error)
description:
Handle invalid JSON in accumulated tool args. Per backend-spec.md §6.5 / §8.2.

When `json.loads(buf["args_str"])` raises JSONDecodeError, emit ToolCallResult(is_error=True, error="invalid json: ...") and continue the loop without invoking the tool. (Already implemented in BACK-A-065 — this issue is the dedicated test + verify behavior.)

RED: fake stream yields tool call with arguments="{not json"; assert ToolCallResult event emitted with is_error=True and error containing "invalid json"; assert no ToolCallEnd; assert loop continues (a subsequent text chunk + RunFinished arrives).
GREEN: confirm logic from 065 covers this; tighten if needed.

title: feat: ChatService — cancellation on client disconnect (no RunError emitted)
description:
Cancel the in-flight LiteLLM call when the client disconnects. Per backend-spec.md §3.3 / §6.2.

run_chat receives `client_disconnected: Callable[[], Awaitable[bool]]` (FastAPI's Request.is_disconnected). Periodically (e.g. between chunks) check it; on True, raise asyncio.CancelledError. Do NOT emit RunError. Log INFO with reason=client_disconnect (logger from §8.3).

  if await client_disconnected():
      logger.info("chat stream cancelled", extra={"run_id": run_id, "reason": "client_disconnect"})
      raise asyncio.CancelledError()

RED: fake client_disconnected returns True after the second chunk. Run run_chat with three chunks queued; assert CancelledError propagates and that no RunError or RunFinished events were emitted after cancellation.
GREEN: wrap chunk handling with the periodic check.

title: feat: ChatService — provider exception → RunError(code, message)
description:
Map provider exceptions into RunError events. Per backend-spec.md §8.2.

Wrap the chunk loop in try/except. On:
  httpx.TimeoutException                     → code="provider_timeout"
  litellm.exceptions.AuthenticationError     → code="provider_auth_failed"
  litellm.exceptions.RateLimitError          → code="provider_rate_limit"
  Exception (last resort)                    → code="internal", log ERROR with traceback

Emit `RunError(run_id, code, message=str(e))` and end the stream.

RED: parametrized tests where fake stream raises each exception; assert exact code values and that RunError is the LAST event before stream end.
GREEN: try/except inside run_chat.

  async def health(self) -> dict[str, Any]:

description:

In src/pypsa_app/llm/api/schemas.py:
  class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
      id: str
      role: Literal["user","assistant","tool"]
      content: str = ""
      tool_call_id: str | None = None
      tool_name:    str | None = None
      timestamp: str
  class ChatContext(BaseModel):
      active_network_id:  str | None = None
      active_network_name: str | None = None
      pinned_network_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
  class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
      messages: list[ChatMessage]
      context: ChatContext = ChatContext()

GREEN: three Pydantic models.

title: feat: get_chat_service / get_llm_client / get_tool_registry deps
feat: POST /api/v1/chat/stream route — wires ChatService.run_chat
description:
Implement POST /api/v1/chat/stream route returning StreamingResponse. Per backend-spec.md §4.2.

In src/pypsa_app/llm/api/routes.py:
  @router.post("/chat/stream", dependencies=[Depends(require_chat_enabled)], response_class=StreamingResponse)
  async def chat_stream(body, request, user, service):
      async def stream():
          try:
              async for sse_bytes in service.run_chat(
                  user=user, messages=body.messages, context=body.context,
                  client_disconnected=request.is_disconnected,
              ):
                  yield sse_bytes
          except asyncio.CancelledError:
              logger.info("chat stream cancelled", extra={"user_id": user.id, "reason": "client_disconnect"})
              raise
      return StreamingResponse(stream(), media_type="text/event-stream", headers=sse_headers)

RED: TestClient with chat_enabled=True, authenticated user, override get_chat_service to a stub yielding RunStarted + TextMessageContent + RunFinished. Assert response.iter_lines() produces those events. Unauthenticated → 401.
GREEN: route handler wired through Depends.

title: feat: types/chat.ts — Role, ToolCall, ToolResult, Message
feat: types/chat.ts — ChatContext + AGUIEvent union
feat: parseSSEBlock — strips comments, joins multiline data, handles bad JSON
description:
Implement parseSSEBlock — pure function that turns one SSE block into an AGUIEvent or null. Per frontend-spec.md §4.

Signature: `function parseSSEBlock(block: string): AGUIEvent | null`

Behavior:
  - Lines starting with `:` are comments (heartbeats) — ignore.
  - Lines starting with `event:` set the event name.
  - Lines starting with `data:` accumulate the JSON payload (multi-line data is `\n`-joined per SSE spec).
  - If event name missing or JSON.parse fails → return null.
  - On success → return `{ event: name, data: JSON.parse(joined) }` cast as AGUIEvent.

RED: tests with these fixtures:
  - Heartbeat-only block (`:keepalive\n\n`) → null
  - Single-line data → parses
  - Multi-line data joined with newline → parses
  - Malformed JSON → null
  - Missing event line → null
GREEN: ~15-line pure function.

title: chore: tests/e2e/compose.yaml — full-stack service + healthcheck
description:
Create tests/e2e/compose.yaml with the full-stack service + healthcheck. Per migration-plan.md §5.3.

The compose file builds the existing root Dockerfile's `full` stage, sets CHAT_ENABLED=true, exposes port 8765 on the host, and waits for /api/v1/health.

  services:
    app:
      build: { context: ../..; dockerfile: Dockerfile; target: full }
      environment:
          CHAT_ENABLED: "true"
          LLM_PROVIDER: openai
          LLM_MODEL: ${LLM_MODEL:-qwen3.5:9b}
          LLM_API_KEY: ${LLM_API_KEY:-}
          LLM_API_BASE: ${LLM_API_BASE:-http://host.docker.internal:11434/v1}
          LLM_MAX_TOKENS: "1024"
          AUTH_ENABLED: "false"
          DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL:-sqlite:////data/test.db}
      ports: ["127.0.0.1:8765:8000"]
      extra_hosts: ["host.docker.internal:host-gateway"]
      healthcheck:
          test: ["CMD","curl","-fs","http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health"]
          interval: 2s; timeout: 2s; retries: 30

RED: a tiny shell script that runs `docker compose -f tests/e2e/compose.yaml config` and asserts the parse succeeds — that is the green-bar.
GREEN: write the file.

title: chore: tests/e2e/run.sh driver + cleanup trap
description:
Create tests/e2e/run.sh — driver script with cleanup trap. Per migration-plan.md §5.4.

  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  set -euo pipefail
  cd "$(dirname "$0")"
  cleanup() { docker compose -f compose.yaml down -v --remove-orphans; }
  trap cleanup EXIT
  echo "[e2e] building + starting app…"
  docker compose -f compose.yaml up -d --build --wait
  PASS=0; FAIL=0
  for t in tests/*.sh; do
      echo "--- $t"
      if bash "$t"; then PASS=$((PASS+1)); echo "    PASS"
      else FAIL=$((FAIL+1)); echo "    FAIL"; fi
  done
  echo "[e2e] passed=$PASS failed=$FAIL"
  [ "$FAIL" -eq 0 ]

RED: a meta-test (or just running ./run.sh with a single trivial test under tests/) that verifies the trap fires and `docker compose down` is called even on test failure.
GREEN: the script + chmod +x.

title: chore: tests/e2e/lib/sse_parse.py + unit tests
test: e2e/tests/01_health.sh
description:
Test 01_health.sh — sanity check /api/v1/health and /api/v1/features. Per migration-plan.md §5.6.

  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  set -euo pipefail
  curl -fs "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/health" >/dev/null
  curl -fs "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/features" | grep -q '"chat_enabled":true'

RED: run against a stack with CHAT_ENABLED=false → expect failure on the grep. CHAT_ENABLED=true → pass.
GREEN: the script.

title: test: e2e/tests/02_chat_disabled_returns_404.sh
description:
Test 02_chat_disabled_returns_404.sh. Per migration-plan.md §5.6.

The harness brings up a second compose service (or restarts with override) where CHAT_ENABLED=false. Then:
  STATUS=$(curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/chat/stream" \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"messages":[],"context":{"active_network_id":null,"active_network_name":null,"pinned_network_ids":[]}}')
  [ "$STATUS" = "404" ]

RED: against an enabled stack, this would return 200 — assert it does NOT. Against disabled, assert 404.
GREEN: the script + a `compose.disabled.yaml` override.

title: test: e2e/tests/03_chat_enabled_basic_stream.sh
description:
Test 03_chat_enabled_basic_stream.sh — sanity event sequence. Per migration-plan.md §5.6.

  EVENTS=$(curl -fsN -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/chat/stream" \
      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
      -d '{"messages":[{"id":"m1","role":"user","content":"Say hello in one word.","timestamp":"2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"}],
           "context":{"active_network_id":null,"active_network_name":null,"pinned_network_ids":[]}}' \
      | python3 lib/sse_parse.py)
  echo "$EVENTS" | jq -se '.[0] | select(.event=="RunStarted")' >/dev/null
  echo "$EVENTS" | jq -se 'map(select(.event=="TextMessageContent")) | length > 0' >/dev/null
  echo "$EVENTS" | jq -se 'map(select(.event=="RunFinished")) | length == 1' >/dev/null

RED: against a stub LLM that returns no events → assertions fail. Against a real LLM → pass.
GREEN: the script.

title: test: e2e/tests/04_chat_tool_call_list_networks.sh
description:
Test 04_chat_tool_call_list_networks.sh — sanity check tool-call event sequence. Per migration-plan.md §5.6.

Send a prompt like "list my networks" (or one engineered to force the tool call against the stub LLM). Assert event sequence contains:
  ToolCallStart with tool_name=="list_networks"
  ToolCallEnd
  ToolCallResult with is_error=false

  echo "$EVENTS" | jq -se 'map(select(.event=="ToolCallStart" and .data.tool_name=="list_networks")) | length > 0' >/dev/null
  echo "$EVENTS" | jq -se 'map(select(.event=="ToolCallResult" and .data.is_error==false)) | length > 0' >/dev/null

RED: stub LLM that doesn't call tools → fails. Stub that returns the tool call → passes.
GREEN: the script.

title: test: e2e/tests/05_chat_abort.sh
description:
Test 05_chat_abort.sh — abort mid-stream and verify server health. Per migration-plan.md §5.6.

Start a long stream in the background, kill curl after ~1 s, then query /api/v1/health to confirm server is fine (no leaked tasks, no 500s). The CancelledError handling in BACK-A-070/086 is what makes this pass.

  curl -fsN -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/chat/stream" -d '...' &
  PID=$!; sleep 1; kill $PID 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep 0.5
  curl -fs "http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/v1/health" >/dev/null

RED: if cancellation isn't handled, server may 500 on subsequent requests → health curl fails.
GREEN: the script.

title: feat: stub-LLM container (FastAPI mimicking OpenAI /v1/chat/completions SSE)
description:
Stub-LLM container — minimal FastAPI app speaking OpenAI /v1/chat/completions SSE. Per migration-plan.md §5.7.

In tests/e2e/stub-llm/main.py:
  from fastapi import FastAPI
  from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
  app = FastAPI()
  @app.post("/v1/chat/completions")
  async def completions(body: dict):
      # Optionally inspect body["messages"][-1]["content"] to decide between
      # a "say hello" response (text only) and a "list networks" response
      # (tool call). Return scripted SSE in OpenAI streaming format.
      ...

The stub must support the subset of fields LiteLLM relies on: streaming chunks with `choices[0].delta.content`, `choices[0].delta.tool_calls`, and `finish_reason`.

RED: a unit test that spins up the stub, sends a request, asserts the SSE stream matches the scripted shape.
GREEN: the FastAPI app + Dockerfile under tests/e2e/stub-llm/.

title: chore: compose override → use stub-LLM when LLM_API_KEY unset
description:
Compose override → use stub-LLM when LLM_API_KEY is unset. Per migration-plan.md §5.7.

In tests/e2e/compose.stub.yaml (or as a profile in compose.yaml):
  services:
    stub-llm:
      build: ./stub-llm
      ports: ["127.0.0.1:8766:8000"]
    app:
      environment:
          LLM_API_BASE: "http://stub-llm:8000/v1"
          LLM_API_KEY: "stub"

run.sh checks: if `[ -z "${LLM_API_KEY:-}" ]`, use `compose.yaml -f compose.stub.yaml`; otherwise use `compose.yaml` alone.

RED: with LLM_API_KEY unset, the harness brings up stub-llm. Test 03 still passes because stub returns the canonical event sequence.
GREEN: override file + run.sh logic.

title: ci: GitHub Actions e2e.yml runs run.sh against stub-LLM
description:
GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/e2e.yml. Per migration-plan.md §5.7.

  name: e2e
  on: [pull_request]
  jobs:
    e2e:
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@v4
          - run: bash tests/e2e/run.sh
            env: { LLM_API_KEY: "" }   # forces stub-LLM path

title: chore: add live pytest marker for provider smoke tests
description:
Add a `live` pytest marker for live-provider smoke tests. Per migration-plan.md §3 / backend-spec.md §9.3.

In pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]:
  markers = [..., "live: requires a live LLM provider (skipped by default)"]

In tests/llm/test_live_provider.py:
  @pytest.mark.live
  def test_qwen_round_trip(): ...

CI runs `pytest -m "not live"`. Local devs run `pytest -m live` only when needed.

RED: pytest --collect-only shows the live test discovered but not run by default.
GREEN: marker registered + sample test.
